name: follow-up-tracker
label: Follow-up Tracker
desc: A continuous loop that watches what happens after something you just shipped and surfaces regressions against a standing objective. Works best pasted right after finishing the task you want tracked.
description: Look at what was just built or shipped in this session and set up a loop that tracks its aftermath. Before creating anything, verify a concrete way to observe the outcome exists on this machine - logs, metrics or MCP tools, a URL to check, or gh - and run it once as a smoke test; if nothing can actually be observed, say so and do not create a blind loop. Define a concrete standing objective. Propose a cadence that fits the thing being tracked (a few runs per day is a common default) and confirm it with me. On each run, observe through the verified path, report what you find (and every declared metric), and keep watching after the target is met so regressions remain visible; the owner decides when to pause or delete the loop. Set up a modest dashboard at creation: an embed of the latest report, plus a metric chart when a metric was defined.
